mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and increases debt-service costs, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward regression of 20-day SPX returns on FEDFUNDS yields a positive or zero slope coefficient over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market that compresses corporate earnings expectations and widens the equity risk premium, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the mean return in those months is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales and industrial production indicates a coordinated contraction in aggregate demand and manufacturing output, which amplifies recession risk and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and INDPRO declines does not significantly underperform the sum of their independent effects on 60-day SPX returns (interaction term F-test p > 0.10) over any 5-year rolling window, or returns are positive in more than 50% of joint-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is above 200 index points and the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy is locked in; further inflation pressure tightens financial conditions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: high inflation with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: In months satisfying CPIAUCSL > 200 and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect accelerating construction activity and positive wealth effects that boost cyclical corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between month-over-month change in HOUST and 20-day SPX forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates improving corporate operating leverage and revenue growth, which the market prices into equity valuations over short horizons, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO change and 5-day SPX forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals contracting aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which triggers downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS declines, or the mean return in those months is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real GDP and retail sales each independently signal broad-based demand strength that supports corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 60-day SPX forward return following joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.
